results.possibleIdentity: Herbert Herman (often referred to as H. Herman) is a Distinguished Professor Emeritus in the Department of Materials Science and Engineering at the State University of New York (SUNY) at Stony Brook. A prominent metallurgist and materials scientist, Herman joined the faculty at Stony Brook in 1968 as an Associate Professor and served as the department chair from 1973 to 1980. He earned his Ph.D. in Metallurgy and Materials Science from Northwestern University in 1961 and was a Fulbright Scholar at the University of Paris. His research primarily focused on metallurgy, surface science, and thermal spray technology, eventually leading him to found and direct the Center for Thermal Spray Research (CTSR) at Stony Brook in 1996. During the late 1960s and early 1970s, he was involved in experimental and collaborative engineering projects, such as the 1972 SUNY-LAB underwater habitat project, which he supervised. This interdisciplinary spirit aligns with the mission of Experiments in Art and Technology (E.A.T.), an organization active during his early tenure at Stony Brook that facilitated collaborations between the technical community and artists. Herman also served as a long-time editor for the influential series 'Treatise on Materials Science and Technology'.
